description: Ancient Egyptians depicted Khepri, the morning form of the sun god, as a dung beetle, which can be seen in the center of this beaded shroud. Just as the sun is born anew each dawn, Egyptians planned to be reborn into a new form of existence after death. As symbols of this renewal, scarab amulets were secured to the body with linen wrappings or incorporated into nets made of beads and laid over a mummified body.
title: Bead Net Funerary Shroud
